task_completion.description: Verify the creation of a dashboard named 'Statistical Traffic Z-Score'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Graphite web interface should display a dashboard with the title 'Statistical Traffic Z-Score' at the top.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Traffic Baseline' graph includes the raw metric
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph titled 'Traffic Baseline' is visible, and its legend or target list includes the raw metric 'servers.web_traffic.speed_sensor_1'.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Traffic Baseline' graph includes the 1-hour moving average
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Traffic Baseline' graph's legend or target list includes the function 'movingAverage(servers.web_traffic.speed_sensor_1, '1h')'.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Traffic Volatility' graph displays the 1-hour rolling standard deviation
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph titled 'Traffic Volatility' is visible, and its legend or target list shows the function 'movingStdDev(servers.web_traffic.speed_sensor_1, '1h')'.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Live Z-Score' graph computes the Z-score using Graphite functions
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph titled 'Live Z-Score' is visible. Its target list or legend shows a nested function computing (X - μ) / σ, which typically looks like 'divide(diffSeries(servers.web_traffic.speed_sensor_1, movingAverage(...)), movingStdDev(...))'.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Live Z-Score' graph includes +3 and -3 Sigma reference lines
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Live Z-Score' graph displays horizontal reference lines at the y-axis values of 3 and -3. The legend should show these were created using the threshold() function, labeled '+3 Sigma' and '-3 Sigma'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Graphite web GUI to create the dashboard
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Graphite web interface (browser) being actively used to add graphs and configure targets, rather than a terminal or text editor modifying JSON files directly.

integrity.description: Verify the graphs are actually rendering data
integrity.visual_evidence: The graphs should display actual plotted lines/curves corresponding to the traffic data, indicating a genuine connection to the metric feed, not just empty graph canvases.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not just type the expected answers into a text file
integrity.visual_evidence: The outputs must be within the Graphite dashboard UI. There should be no text editors open just displaying the task requirements or expected function strings as a shortcut.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected dashboard name is exactly 'Statistical Traffic Z-Score'. The exact metric to be used across all graphs is 'servers.web_traffic.speed_sensor_1'. The Z-score computation in Graphite requires combining functions to achieve (X - μ) / σ, typically using divide(), diffSeries(), movingAverage(), and movingStdDev().
